text:
On average, a person shares about 50% of their genes with both their full siblings and each of their parents. This is because:

- You inherit half of your DNA from your mother and half from your father (50% each).
- Your full sibling also gets half from each parent, but not necessarily the same half, so you and your sibling share about 50% of your genetic material on average.

So, you share roughly the same proportion of your genes—about 50%—with your full siblings as you do with each of your parents.
